business.headline: Sensex opens 600 points lower, Nifty below 23,500; Tata Steel falls 2%Sensex opens 600 points lower, Nifty below 23,500; Tata Steel falls 2%

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market rise or fall today?Stock markets are set for a weaker open as rising crude, higher US yields and Iran tensions weigh on sentiment. Persistent foreign selling, elevated volatility and key technical levels are keeping traders cautious.

Wait 5 years for gratuity or take the pay hike? Here's what Indian workers saidYou are just months away from gratuity when a recruiter calls with a tempting pay hike. Do you wait for guaranteed money, or take the career leap? To understand what people really choose, I spoke to Indian professionals across sectors, and the answers were surprisingly divided.

ICICI Bank hikes debit card DCC fee from 1% to 3.5%: What it meansDynamic Currency Conversion, commonly called DCC, allows customers to pay in their home currency instead of the local currency while making international transactions.

3 in 10 loan applications show ITR-bank mismatch: Can you still get a personal loan?Your ITR says one thing, but your bank statement tells a slightly different story — should you worry? As lenders tighten checks and nearly 3 in 10 loan applications show income mismatches, can you still get a personal loan without trouble? Let's find out here.

Why foreign investors are shunning India: Surjit Bhalla points out root causeEven as India's economy faces mounting pressure from rising crude oil prices and disruptions in fertiliser and energy supplies, economist and author Surjit Bhalla has identified a worsening investment climate as the country's biggest challenge.

Sensex, Nifty end lower as banking drag offsets IT rally; rupee hits record lowThe S&P BSE Sensex slipped 114.19 points, or 0.15%, to close at 75,200.85, while the NSE Nifty50 fell 31.95 points, or 0.14%, to settle at 23,618.

Govt likely to receive a big dividend from RBI in FY 27The Reserve Bank of India is expected to approve a record surplus transfer to the government this week. The payout could give the exchequer extra room as higher energy prices add fresh uncertainty.

Want to update Aadhaar for free? UIDAI has extended the deadline againThe UIDAI has announced that Aadhaar holders can continue updating documents linked to their Aadhaar card free of cost through the myAadhaar portal till June 14, 2027. The facility was earlier set to end in June 2026.

Rupee hits record low yet again, nears Rs 97 against US dollarThe rupee slipped to a fresh record low of 96.44 against the US dollar on Tuesday, nearing the Rs 97 mark. Rising crude prices, weak capital inflows and higher US yields are adding to pressure on India's external finances despite RBI intervention.

As milk, fuel and gold get costlier, here are 7 everyday expenses families may cutThe grocery bill feels heavier. Filling the petrol tank costs more. Even an occasional gold purchase suddenly seems expensive. Across India, rising prices are pinching household budgets, leaving many families asking: What everyday expenses can we cut without making life harder? Let's have a look.

Before you file your ITR this year, don't miss these details in AIS and Form 26ASAre you about to file your income tax return without checking AIS and Form 26AS? Before you hit submit, it may be worth taking a closer look, as even small mismatches in income or tax details can create problems later.

Infosys, TCS, Tech Mahindra shares jump: Why IT stocks gained up to 6% todayMajor IT stocks rebounded sharply on Tuesday, led by Infosys, TCS and midcap peers. The move reflected improving risk appetite, bargain hunting and renewed hopes of stronger US tech spending.

Explained: Why Adani Group stocks rose up to 5% todayAdani Group shares climbed in morning trade after the US moved to drop charges against Gautam Adani and his associates. The move, along with the LPG settlement, eased regulatory uncertainty and lifted investor confidence.

How the West Asia war triggered a global fight for India's oil marketRussia, Gulf producers and the United States are competing harder for India's oil market as the West Asia war disrupts trade routes. The scramble underlines India's growing leverage as one of the few big economies where oil demand is still rising.

Petrol, diesel prices rise again: Will 2022's 90-paise hike formula return?State-run oil companies have raised petrol and diesel prices again across major cities. The move has revived talk of a 2022-style pattern of small, frequent fuel hikes.

Sensex opens 300 points higher, Nifty above 23,700; Infosys up 4%The S&P BSE Sensex jumped 365.07 points, or 0.48%, to 75,680.11 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 107.55 points, or 0.45%, to 23,757.50, as of 9:40 am.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Stock market to crash after 2nd hike in fuel prices?Markets are set for a mildly positive open even after a second fuel price hike in a week. Hopes of easing tensions around Iran are offering support, but high crude prices and volatility are keeping traders cautious.

Standard Chartered plans 7,000 job cuts amid AI push and higher profit targetsStandard Chartered said it will cut more than 7,000 roles by 2030 as it leans on automation and artificial intelligence. The bank is tying the move to higher return targets and a bigger push in wealth management.

8th Pay Commission: The food-cost formula behind salary revision demandsEmployee unions have asked the 8th Pay Commission to recalculate minimum pay using updated food-cost and nutrition standards. The push shows how salary revision talks are increasingly centred on inflation, household budgets and daily living costs.

entertainment.headline: Dragon teaser X reviews: Fans call Jr NTR-Prashanth Neel combo 'insane' The teaser of Dragon starring Jr NTR has sparked massive reactions online after its release on the actor's birthday. Fans especially praised the Jr NTR-Prashanth Neel collaboration, with many calling the combo "insane."

Aditi Rao Hydari serves quiet luxury in cream look at Cannes 2026 Aditi Rao Hydari arrived at Cannes Film Festival 2026 in a cream Club L London midi dress styled by Sanam Ratansi. The actor shared several pictures and videos of the talked-about look on social media.

Haven't forgotten how to fight: Salman Khan shreds pap conduct after hospital chaos Salman Khan reacted strongly after photographers followed him to Mumbai's Hinduja Hospital. He slammed paparazzi behaviour and said he has "not forgotten how to fight."

Why Mohanlal's Drishyam 3 is Malayalam cinema's biggest box office test in 2026 Drishyam 3 is more than a sequel for Malayalam cinema. Here are five reasons why Mohanlal's biggest release in years could shape the industry's box office ambitions in 2026.

Grey's Anatomy gets a new spinoff series, set to explore rural healthcare Grey's Anatomy is expanding its universe with a brand-new spinoff set in rural Texas. The upcoming drama, created by Shonda Rhimes and Meg Marinis, will focus on a medical team serving remote communities in West Texas.

Rhea Chakraborty announces social media break, says online noise felt heavy Rhea Chakraborty announced a temporary break from social media, saying the constant noise and pressure online started feeling overwhelming. The actor shared that she now wants to focus on reconnecting with herself and choosing "lived moments over posted ones."

Jr NTR is Dragon, unleashes fury in gritty 1st look set in pre-independence era The makers of NTRNeel unveiled a new Jr NTR poster before the film's first glimpse launch. The release has sharpened fan curiosity around Prashanth Neel's world ahead of NTR's birthday reveal.

Salman Khan schools paps, urges silence outside hospital in viral clip. Watch Salman Khan was seen asking photographers to stay quiet outside Hinduja Hospital in Mumbai after they followed him, with videos of the incident now circulating on social media.

Through storm and silence: Actor Darshan's wife marks anniversary after jail visit Kannada actor Darshan's wife shared a moving anniversary post about strength and storm hours after meeting him in Bengaluru's jail. The couple marked their 23rd wedding anniversary on May 19.

Twisha Sharma's co-actor Swetaa Varma reacts to her death, seeks fair probe Swetaa Varma has expressed shock over Twisha Sharma's death and called for a proper investigation. The case has widened after police booked Sharma's husband and mother-in-law and an SIT began its probe.

Kejriwal's dance to deleted photos: Singer-priest recalls Raghav-Parineeti wedding Vedic priest Ankit Batra shared new details from Parineeti Chopra and Raghav Chadha's Udaipur wedding. He revealed that Arvind Kejriwal danced in the baraat, while Madhu Chopra was asked to delete pictures from her phone.

Shikhar Pahariya cheers for Janhvi Kapoor after Peddi trailer, calls her 'lady don' Businessman Shikhar Pahariya reacted to the Peddi trailer and called girlfriend Janhvi Kapoor a 'lady don'. He also extended his love to the film's lead, Ram Charan.

Sonakshi Sinha, Jyotika on male stars being treated like 'God' on film sets Ahead of System's release, Sonakshi Sinha and Jyotika spoke to India Today about gender disparity on film sets, workplace bias and pay parity in the industry. The actors also described the film as a realistic courtroom and family drama centred on strong female characters.

Peddi director shuts down viral theory about Ram Charan losing a leg in film Ever since the Peddi trailer dropped, social media has been flooded with theories about Ram Charan's character suffering a shocking fate in the film. Director Buchi Babu Sana has now stepped in to address the speculation, while carefully holding back the real surprises.

Rishab Shetty reviews 'spectacular' Peddi trailer, calls Ram Charan absolute fire Rishab Shetty is all in for the Peddi trailer. Sharing the trailer on Instagram, the Kannada superstar showered love on Ram Charan, Shivarajkumar and other cast. The sports drama, directed by Buchi Babu Sana, will release globally on June 4.

Diljit Dosanjh says 'main bhi vaapas aaunga' after skipping trailer launch. Video Diljit Dosanjh missed the trailer launch of Main Vaapas Aaunga in Mumbai and addressed the audience through a video message. The upcoming film also stars Sharvari and Vedang Raina.

No one is coming to save you: Kangana to married women after Noida, Bhopal deaths Kangana Ranaut shared a note on the struggles faced by married women amid the Noida and Bhopal cases. She urged young women to prioritise financial independence before marriage.

Kamal Haasan skips luxury travel, takes economy flight to save costs for Kalki 2 Kamal Haasan has resumed shooting for the sequel to Kalki 2898 AD and travelled economy class to reach the set. The move was praised by Vyjayanthi Movies as it aligns with his recent call to cut avoidable production costs.

Actor Lawrence backs CM Vijay's decision to give film portfolio to MLA Rajmohan Actor-director Raghava Lawrence has defended Chief Minister Vijay's decision to give Egmore MLA Rajmohan the film technology and cinematograph portfolio. His remarks call for patience as Rajmohan takes charge amid rising concerns within the Tamil film industry.

general news.headline: Norwegian daily under fire for racist cartoon of PM Modi as snake charmerA Norwegian newspaper sparked outrage after publishing a cartoon depicting Prime Minister Narendra Modi as a snake charmer amid a row over a journalist trying to question him at a press briefing.

BJP govt will be removed from Centre in coming days: Mamata BanerjeeSpeaking at a meeting of TMC MLAs at her residence, Mamata alleged that minority communities and roadside hawkers are being targeted by the newly formed BJP government in West Bengal.

US set up a chessboard with Munir as a pawn years ago. It's playing out nowA chessboard had been laid much earlier. A game had begun even before former Pakistan PM Imran Khan realised it, and Asim Munir took over as the army chief. The US moved the pieces for a regime change in Pakistan. It now has a strategically placed player as the Middle East burns.

Meta layoffs begin, employees wake up to 4 am email, 8,000 jobs on the lineMeta has reportedly begun laying off workers globally, with around 8,000 employees expected to be impacted. As per reports, the company sent emails to affected employees in its Singapore hub at 4 am on Wednesday.

I won't work for them: The dangerous normalisation of communal politicsStatements that would once have ended political careers are now shrugged off as routine election rhetoric. Open religious mobilisation is no longer whispered from the shadows. It is increasingly performed in broad daylight.

Video from Tamil Nadu CM Vijay's meeting questions 'reel culture' in governanceA video from Vijay's meeting with disability rights representatives triggered allegations that a government official had filmed and shared it as a reel. Clarifications later said the footage was recorded by social worker Dr P Simmachandran with Vijay's permission.

Car ride, dinner, selfie at the Colosseum: A Melodi evening in RomePrime Minister Narendra Modi arrived in Rome on the final leg of his five-nation tour and received a warm welcome from Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni.

US, Israel eyed Ahmadinejad to lead Iran after war. Here's why the plot failedDays after strikes killed Ali Khamenei, Donald Trump said Iran should be led by someone from within. Washington and Israel had Mahmoud Ahmadinejad in mind, sharpening questions over Iran's succession and the widening war.

Why Mohanlal's Drishyam 3 is Malayalam cinema's biggest box office test in 2026Drishyam 3 is more than a sequel for Malayalam cinema. Here are five reasons why Mohanlal's biggest release in years could shape the industry's box office ambitions in 2026.

India's twin submarine dilemmaTwo crucial pieces of the puzzle — a nuclear-powered attack submarine and an indigenously designed and built conventional submarine — have been missing from the Indian Navy's force structure.

Small price to pay: Norwegian journalist says Insta, FB accounts suspendedNorwegian journalist Helle Lyng said her Instagram and Facebook accounts were suspended. She called it a "small price to pay for press freedom".

Trump says US could strike Iran again within days as Tehran warns of 'new fronts'Donald Trump said the US could strike Iran within days if talks fail, after pausing action to allow diplomacy. Iran warned it would open new fronts in response, as the US and Israel prepared for possible renewed operations.

Everyone likes a NEET paper leak: Students spend Rs 35,000 as coaching hubs cash inWho is paying the price for the NEET paper leak? Certainly not the system that failed. Instead, millions of exhausted students and financially stretched families are once again being pushed into India's coaching machine, where fear, uncertainty and desperation are rapidly turning into fresh business opportunities. For these 37 days, students are paying up Rs 35,000 on average for crash courses, special re-NEET classes, and hostels.

North India to burn, South to be drenched: What Wednesday's weather looks likeThe seasonal rains that irrigate India's farms and fill its reservoirs are advancing steadily and are expected to set in over Kerala several days ahead of the usual June 1 date.

5.9-magnitude earthquake hits Japan's Kagoshima, no tsunami warning for nowA 5.9-magnitude earthquake struck Japan's Kagoshima region on Wednesday, though no immediate reports of casualties or major damage emerged.

Why TMC's 'Pushpa' Jahangir Khan bowed out of Falta repoll raceJahangir Khan withdrew from the Falta repoll days before voting. His exit has intensified focus on the TMC's silence and the politics around Abhishek Banerjee's turf.

India's squad vs Afghanistan: Decoding big snubs, what it means for the futureThe squad announcement for the Afghanistan series marks the official blueprint for India's future. While senior pillars remain, the selectors have actively initiated a transition phase, blooding a younger core ahead of the 2027 World Cup cycle.

Video: Moment US cargo plane lost engine before fiery crash that killed 15The NTSB has released CCTV footage showing UPS Flight 2976 losing its left engine and pylon during take-off from Louisville. The video has brought fresh focus to the Kentucky crash in which 15 people died.

Haven't forgotten how to fight: Salman Khan shreds pap conduct after hospital chaosSalman Khan reacted strongly after photographers followed him to Mumbai's Hinduja Hospital. He slammed paparazzi behaviour and said he has "not forgotten how to fight."

TCS employees say salary reduced after appraisal despite 5 percent hikeEmployees at TCS have raised concerns after some claimed their salaries had been reduced despite the company announcing average hikes of 5 per cent. Workers have blamed changes in CTC structure, lower variable pay, and revised appraisal calculations for the confusion.

The journalist is not the story! A shout is not a question!Norwegian journalist Helle Lyng openly admitted on social media that she never expected PM Modi to answer her shouted query. She knew the protocol. The narrative ceased to be about India's press freedom landscape. It became entirely about her.

After The Kashmir Files and The Bengal Files, now comes The Maharashtra FilesThe Maharashtra Files trailer sets up a tense battle between farmers and the powerful people. The Marathi political thriller releases in theatres on May 29, 2026.

Twisha Sharma death: CCTV footage of final 60 minutes raises fresh questionsInvestigators are examining CCTV footage from the building where Twisha Sharma was found unconscious after going to the terrace. The visuals and her final call have sharpened questions over the unexplained hour before she was brought downstairs.

CBI arrests Army colonel in Rs 50 lakh military tender bribery caseAccording to the FIR registered by CBI, directors of a Kanpur-based company allegedly paid bribes to secure Army tenders. The central agency claims that a major tender was awarded to the firm earlier this year in return for bribes.

sports.headline: India's squad vs Afghanistan: Decoding big snubs, what it means for the future The squad announcement for the Afghanistan series marks the official blueprint for India's future. While senior pillars remain, the selectors have actively initiated a transition phase, blooding a younger core ahead of the 2027 World Cup cycle.

Arsenal crowned Premier League champions: The 22-year wait is finally over Arsenal are Premier League champions, ending a 22-year drought. Mikel Arteta's side clinched the title after Manchester City's 1-1 draw at Bournemouth, sparking wild celebrations ahead of Sunday's coronation clash at Crystal Palace.

Play of the Day: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi 2.0 stuns LSG with controlled aggression IPL 2026, RR vs LSG: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi scored 93 runs off 38 balls against the Lucknow Super Giants on Tuesday in Jaipur, but during his innings, he also displayed maturity and patience, revealing another side to his game.

Rishabh Pant drops the F-bomb on live TV: LSG are a f***g good team IPL 2026: Rishabh Pant backed Lucknow Super Giants after their loss to Rajasthan Royals and used an expletive on live television. The remark came as Lucknow slipped to the bottom of the table and Pant faced mounting pressure.

IPL playoff scenario: Rajasthan Royals keep fate in own hands for 4th spot IPL 2026, RR vs LSG: Rajasthan Royals have made life difficult for the other teams in playoff contention after defeating Lucknow Super Giants by seven wickets on Tuesday, May 19 at the Sawai Mansingh Stadium in Jaipur.

RR ride the Vaibhav Sooryavanshi storm to crush LSG and tighten playoff grip IPL 2026: Rajasthan Royals chased 221 against Lucknow Super Giants on the back of Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's 93 and Dhruv Jurel's unbeaten 53. The win kept RR in the playoff race, lifted them above Punjab Kings and left Mitchell Marsh's 96 in vain.

Chris Gayle, watch out: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi first Indian with 50 sixes in IPL season IPL 2026, RR vs LSG: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi became the first Indian to hit 50 sixes in an Indian Premier League season and is closing in on Chris Gayle's long-standing record for most sixes in a single edition.

Where's your chit now? Sunil Gavaskar mocks LSG's Aakash Singh on live TV IPL 2026: Sunil Gavaskar criticised Lucknow Super Giants pacer Aakash Singh during the IPL 2026 clash against Rajasthan Royals after the youngster conceded heavily while defending 220 in Jaipur. Gavaskar questioned Singh's on-field celebrations as teenage batter Vaibhav Sooryavanshi powered RR's chase with a stunning 38-ball 93.

Karman Kaur Thandi crashes out of French Open in horror Grand Slam comeback French Open 2026: Karman Kaur Thandi endured a difficult outing on Tuesday as Harmony Tan needed just 57 minutes to secure a dominant 6-1, 6-0 win in the opening round of the qualifiers.

Mohammad Rizwan Pakistan's last hope as Bangladesh near Test series sweep BAN vs PAK, 2nd Test: Mohammad Rizwan remained unbeaten on 75 as Pakistan are looking to chase 437 in the Sylhet Test, still needing 121 runs for victory, with Bangladesh firmly in control and strong favourites to win the match.

Struggling Carlos Alcaraz pulls out of Wimbledon after missing French Open World No. 2 Carlos Alcaraz confirmed that he will miss the upcoming Wimbledon Championships, scheduled to begin in June, as he continues his recovery from a wrist injury. The Spaniard announced his withdrawal from this year's tournament due to the lingering issue.

Virat Kohli urges next generation to chase greatness beyond instant IPL fameStar Indian batter Virat Kohli urged young cricketers not to get carried away by the instant fame and financial rewards of the IPL, saying the real challenge lies in staying committed to the sport for a long period and earning respect across the cricketing world.

Are IPL teams forcing injured stars to play? BCCI secretary breaks silenceIPL 2026: BCCI secretary Devajit Saikia said franchises make the final call on injured players during the IPL. His remarks followed scrutiny over KKR playing Varun Chakravarthy despite a hairline fracture.

England Women's T20 World Cup scare: Sciver-Brunt ruled out of warm-up seriesNat Sciver-Brunt will miss England's T20 series against New Zealand and India after a calf tear. The injury disrupts a crucial World Cup build-up and forces further changes to England's squad.

Never back down: PV Sindhu reveals priceless advice from Virat Kohli on special meetIndia's badminton star PV Sindhu revealed the inspiring advice she received from Virat Kohli during their recent meeting in Bengaluru, praising the RCB star for constantly guiding and motivating fellow athletes.

Big blow for RR as injured Riyan Parag misses LSG clashIPL 2026: Riyan Parag missed Rajasthan Royals' clash against Lucknow Super Giants with a hamstring injury. His absence, along with Ravindra Jadeja's continued recovery, has added to RR's playoff worries.

Why Rishabh Pant was removed as India's Test vice-captainAjit Agarkar said Rishabh Pant remains central to India's Test plans despite losing the vice-captaincy and his ODI place. The selectors have split his role for now, with KL Rahul elevated and other wicketkeeping options preferred in one-day cricket.

Virat Kohli reveals impostor syndrome, says Rahul Dravid took care of him mentallyFormer India captain Virat Kohli has opened up about battling "impostor syndrome" during his career, revealing that former head coach Rahul Dravid and batting coach Vikram Rathour played a major role in helping him rediscover the joy of playing cricket after stepping down from captaincy.

Cristiano Ronaldo set for record sixth FIFA World Cup as Portugal announce squadCristiano Ronaldo has been picked in Portugal's 27-man squad for what would be his sixth World Cup. The call keeps his chase for a first world title alive and adds an emotional Diogo Jota tribute.

RR vs LSG Highlight: Sooryavanshi-led RR tighten play-off grip with 221 chaseRR (Rajasthan) vs LSG (Lucknow) HighlightScore, IPL 2026: RR (225/3 in 19.1 ovs) chasing 221. A Vaibhav Sooryavanshi storm in Jaipur ensured Rajasthan Royals remained in firm contention of the final play-off birth.

Total shame: Auqib Nabi omission from Afghanistan Test leaves fans furiousIND vs AFG: Auqib Nabi's omission from India's Afghanistan Test squad sparked outrage among fans despite the Jammu and Kashmir pacer enjoying a sensational domestic season.

Days after getting Virat Kohli on a duck, Prince Yadav earns maiden ODI call-upPrince Yadav has earned a first India ODI call-up after a strong domestic and IPL run. This comes amid Virat Kohli's praise of the young pacer and India's rotation call underlining a shift towards testing new pace depth.

Has India moved on from Mohammed Shami? Agarkar casts doubt on pacer's returnAjit Agarkar said Mohammed Shami was not considered for India's longer-format squads against Afghanistan. The remark has sharpened questions over Shami's place in India's ODI and Test plans.

Malaysia Masters 2026: Arjun, Hariharan avenge Thomas Cup loss to Popov brothersHariharan Amsakarunan and M R Arjun beat Christo and Toma Junior Popov in straight games at the Malaysia Masters. The win offered a sharp response after India's Thomas Cup semi-final loss to France earlier this month.

Who is Gurnoor Brar? 6ft 3in Punjab pacer earns maiden India Test call-upPunjab pacer Gurnoor Brar has earned his maiden India Test call-up for the one-off match against Afghanistan next month following a string of impressive performances in domestic cricket and the IPL. The right-arm fast bowler's consistent rise over the last two seasons has now brought him to the doorstep of an international debut.

Rohit Sharma picked subject to fitness, Ishan Kishan back in ODI squadIndia vs Afghanistan: The BCCI announced a 15-member squad, led by Shubman Gill. While Rohit Sharma and Hardik Pandya have been named despite fitness concerns, Rishabh Pant has lost his place.

KL Rahul replaces Rishabh Pant as vice-captain as BCCI announces Test squadThe BCCI on Tuesday announced India's squad for the one-off Test against Afghanistan, with KL Rahul replacing Rishabh Pant as the vice-captain of the Shubman Gill-led side.

How RCB avoided title hangover in IPL 2026: Mo Bobat explains the hunter mindsetMo Bobat said RCB adopted a hunter mindset to reset after last season's title win. He said that approach has helped the side stay driven through a strong IPL 2026 campaign.

MI hit with double blow as star batter, all-rounder ruled out of IPL 2026Mumbai Indians have ruled out Quinton de Kock and Raj Angad Bawa for the rest of IPL 2026 due to injury setbacks. Already out of playoff contention, MI will finish their campaign with matches against Kolkata Knight Riders and Rajasthan Royals.

Ashwin to angry CSK fans: Enjoy Ishan Kishan celebration, why bring ego into it?IPL 2026: Ravichandran Ashwin asked CSK fans not to take Ishan Kishan's Chepauk celebration personally after SRH's win. He said sport needs emotion and drama, though such exchanges should stay respectful.

India squad in good shape for Women's T20 World Cup: Smriti MandhanaVice-captain Smriti Mandhana on Tuesday avoided revealing too much about the Indian women's team's preparations for next month's T20 World Cup, but said the squad is in good condition ahead of the 12-team event.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
